questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for deer crossing the road
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right then left turns ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What must you do at a steady red traffic light?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Come to a complete stop and wait for green
Stop only if other cars are present
Yield to traffic and then continue
questions.explanation: A steady red light means you must stop completely behind the stop line and remain stopped until the light turns green.

questions.prompt: A flashing red traffic light means the same as which sign?
questions.explanation: A flashing red light has the same meaning as a stop sign: stop, yield, and proceed only when it is safe.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light mean?
choices: Speed up to clear the intersection
The light is about to turn red, so stop if you safely can
The light is broken, so proceed carefully
questions.explanation: A steady yellow light warns that the signal is about to turn red, so you should stop if you can do so safely.

questions.prompt: What shape is a stop sign?
questions.explanation: A stop sign is always a red eight-sided octagon, a shape used for no other sign so you can recognize it instantly.

questions.prompt: What color are signs that warn of construction or maintenance zones?
questions.explanation: Orange signs mark construction and road-maintenance zones to warn drivers of workers and changing conditions ahead.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing yellow traffic light mean?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
The light is out of service
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light tells you to slow down and proceed carefully through the intersection.

questions.prompt: A downward-pointing triangular sign tells you to do what?
questions.explanation: A downward-pointing triangle is a yield sign, meaning you must slow down and give the right-of-way to other traffic.

questions.prompt: What does a solid double yellow center line mean?
choices: You may pass when the road is clear
Passing is not allowed in either direction
The lane is for turning only
questions.explanation: A double solid yellow line marks the center of a two-way road and means passing is prohibited for traffic in both directions.

questions.prompt: A green arrow signal pointing left means what?
choices: You may turn left and oncoming traffic is stopped
You must yield to oncoming traffic before turning
questions.explanation: A green left-turn arrow gives you a protected turn, meaning oncoming traffic is stopped and you may turn safely.

questions.prompt: What do white lane lines on a roadway separate?
choices: Lanes of traffic moving in opposite directions
Lanes of traffic moving in the same direction
questions.explanation: White lines separate lanes of traffic traveling in the same direction, while yellow lines separate opposing traffic.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, two cars arrive at the same time. Who goes first?
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop at the same time, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When turning left at an intersection, you must yield to whom?
choices: Oncoming traffic and pedestrians in the crosswalk
No one if you have a green light
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must yield to oncoming vehicles and to pedestrians crossing the road you are turning into.

questions.prompt: In Florida, when may you turn right on a red light?
choices: After a complete stop, if no sign prohibits it and the way is clear
Only if a green arrow is showing
questions.explanation: You may turn right on red after a full stop, unless a sign prohibits it, and only after yielding to pedestrians and cross traffic.

questions.prompt: What must you do when you approach a blind pedestrian using a white cane or guide dog?
choices: Come to a full stop and yield
questions.explanation: You must come to a complete stop and yield the right-of-way to a blind pedestrian using a white cane or guide dog.

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren on, you should:
choices: Speed up to get out of the way
Pull over to the right edge and stop
questions.explanation: You must pull over to the right edge of the road and stop until the emergency vehicle has passed.

questions.prompt: How far before a turn should you signal your intention in Florida?
questions.explanation: Signaling at least 100 feet before a turn gives other drivers time to react to your intended movement.

questions.prompt: Who has the right-of-way at an unmarked crosswalk?
choices: The driver, because it is unmarked
questions.explanation: You must yield to pedestrians in any crosswalk, whether it is marked with paint or unmarked at an intersection.

questions.prompt: When entering a roadway from a driveway or parking lot, you must:
choices: Yield to traffic already on the road
Expect other drivers to slow for you
questions.explanation: A driver entering a road from a driveway or private property must yield to vehicles and pedestrians already using the road.

questions.prompt: At an intersection without signals or signs, who must yield?
choices: The vehicle approaching from the right
The vehicle approaching from the left
questions.explanation: At an uncontrolled intersection, the driver on the left must yield to the driver approaching from the right.

questions.prompt: When you reach a roundabout, you should:
choices: Stop before entering even if it is clear
Yield to traffic already in the circle
Enter quickly to keep traffic moving
questions.explanation: Vehicles entering a roundabout must yield to traffic already circulating inside it.

questions.prompt: In Florida, what is the default speed limit in a business or residential district when none is posted?
questions.explanation: Florida law sets a default speed limit of 30 mph in business and residential districts unless a different limit is posted.

questions.prompt: What is the default speed limit on most rural Florida roads outside of cities when no sign is posted?
questions.explanation: Outside business and residential districts, Florida's default speed limit on rural roads is 55 mph.

questions.prompt: What is the maximum speed limit on Florida interstate highways?
questions.explanation: Florida sets a maximum speed limit of 70 mph on interstate highways unless a lower limit is posted.

questions.prompt: A good rule for a safe following distance behind the car ahead is at least:
questions.explanation: Staying at least three to four seconds behind the vehicle ahead gives you time to stop safely, and you should add more in bad conditions.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic and blocking its flow is:
questions.explanation: Driving too slowly and impeding the normal flow of traffic is illegal and can be as dangerous as speeding.

questions.prompt: In rain, fog, or heavy traffic, you should:
choices: Reduce your speed below the posted limit
Increase your speed to pass quickly
questions.explanation: You must always drive at a speed reasonable for conditions, which often means slowing well below the posted limit.

questions.prompt: You should increase your following distance when:
choices: The road is dry and clear
Following a motorcycle or large truck
questions.explanation: Add following distance behind motorcycles and large trucks, and in poor weather or at night, to give yourself more time to react.

questions.prompt: What is the main danger of tailgating?
choices: It is required in heavy traffic
questions.explanation: Following too closely leaves no room to stop and is a leading cause of rear-end crashes.

questions.prompt: The safest way to slow down for a stop ahead is to:
choices: Brake hard at the last moment
questions.explanation: Braking early and gradually is safer than hard last-second braking, which can cause skids and rear-end crashes.

questions.prompt: Why should you avoid driving in another vehicle's blind spot?
choices: The other driver may not see you and could change lanes into you
questions.explanation: Lingering in a blind spot is dangerous because the other driver cannot see you and may move into your lane.

questions.prompt: On a two-lane road, you may legally pass on the left when:
choices: The center line is a solid yellow on your side
The center line is broken and the oncoming lane is clear
questions.explanation: You may pass on the left only where the center line is broken on your side and the oncoming lane is clear.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane highway, the left lane is generally used for:
questions.explanation: Slower traffic should keep right, and the left lane is generally reserved for passing on multi-lane roads.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Signal, check mirrors, and look over your shoulder
questions.explanation: Signal, check your mirrors, and glance over your shoulder to check the blind spot before moving into another lane.

questions.prompt: When may you cross a solid yellow line on your side of the road to pass?
choices: When you are in a hurry
When the car ahead is slow
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means passing is prohibited; you must not cross it to pass another vehicle.

questions.prompt: You should not attempt to pass when approaching a:
choices: Hill or curve that blocks your view
questions.explanation: Never pass near a hill, curve, intersection, or railroad crossing where you cannot see oncoming traffic in time.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, you should return to your lane when:
choices: You are even with the vehicle
You can see the passed vehicle in your rear-view mirror
The other driver waves you in
questions.explanation: Return to your lane only after you can see the vehicle you passed in your rear-view mirror, giving it enough room.

questions.prompt: On a two-way street, what must you do when a school bus stops with its red lights flashing?
questions.explanation: On a two-way road without a raised barrier, traffic in both directions must stop for a school bus with flashing red lights.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane road, slower-moving vehicles should:
choices: Drive in the center lane only
questions.explanation: Keeping right except to pass allows faster traffic to flow safely in the left lanes.

questions.prompt: When may you legally pass a vehicle on the right?
choices: On a road with two or more lanes in your direction when it is safe
Whenever the car ahead is slow
questions.explanation: Passing on the right is allowed only on roads with two or more lanes going your way and when it can be done safely.

questions.prompt: Under Florida's Move Over Law, if you cannot safely change lanes for a stopped emergency vehicle, you must:
choices: Slow to 20 mph below the posted speed limit
questions.explanation: If you cannot move over, the Move Over Law requires you to slow to 20 mph below the posted limit (or 5 mph if the limit is 20 mph or less).

questions.prompt: When passing a bicyclist, Florida law requires you to give at least:
questions.explanation: Drivers must give bicyclists at least three feet of clearance when passing them.

questions.prompt: Why are motorcycles easy to miss in traffic?
choices: They are smaller and harder to see
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are small and can be hidden in blind spots, so look carefully before turning or changing lanes.

questions.prompt: Large trucks have large blind spots, so you should:
questions.explanation: Trucks cannot see well on all four sides, so avoid lingering beside them and never cut in front and brake.

questions.prompt: The Move Over Law applies to which stopped vehicles displaying flashing lights?
choices: Emergency, law enforcement, utility, sanitation, and disabled vehicles
questions.explanation: Florida's Move Over Law protects emergency, law enforcement, utility, sanitation, and disabled vehicles stopped with lights flashing.

questions.prompt: Where should you look extra carefully for motorcycles?
questions.explanation: Many car-motorcycle crashes happen at intersections, so check twice for motorcycles before turning or crossing.

questions.prompt: When you see pedestrians near a school zone, you should:
choices: Slow down and be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Reduce speed and stay alert in school zones, where children may step into the road unexpectedly.

questions.prompt: A large truck needs more room than a car to:
questions.explanation: Large trucks need extra distance to stop and extra room to make turns, so give them space.

questions.prompt: If a truck is making a wide right turn, you should:
choices: Wait and not try to pass on the right
Honk and force your way through
Pass on the left at high speed
questions.explanation: Trucks often swing left before turning right, so never try to pass on the right side of a turning truck.

questions.prompt: In Florida, what is the legal BAC limit for a driver who is 21 or older?
questions.explanation: It is illegal to drive in Florida with a blood alcohol concentration of 0.08 percent or higher if you are 21 or older.

questions.prompt: Under Florida's zero-tolerance law, what BAC will cause a driver under 21 to lose their license?
questions.explanation: Drivers under 21 with a BAC of 0.02 percent or higher face an automatic six-month license suspension under the zero-tolerance law.

questions.prompt: In Florida, texting while driving is:
choices: A primary offense you can be stopped for
questions.explanation: Texting while driving is a primary offense in Florida, so an officer can pull you over and ticket you for it alone.

questions.prompt: How does alcohol affect your driving ability?
choices: It slows reaction time and impairs judgment
It has no effect in small amounts
questions.explanation: Alcohol slows your reactions, blurs vision, and reduces judgment, and impairment begins with the very first drink.

questions.prompt: Which of the following can impair your ability to drive safely?
questions.explanation: Many prescription and over-the-counter medicines can cause drowsiness or impairment, so read warning labels before driving.

questions.prompt: What is the best way to handle your phone while driving?
choices: Put it away and keep your attention on the road
questions.explanation: Put your phone away and keep your full attention on driving, since distraction is a leading cause of crashes.

questions.prompt: The safest amount of alcohol to drink before driving is:
choices: As long as you are under the limit
questions.explanation: There is no safe amount of alcohol to drink before driving because impairment begins with the first drink.

questions.prompt: Taking your eyes off the road to read a text at highway speed means you may travel:
choices: About the length of a football field nearly blind
questions.explanation: At highway speed, a few seconds of looking away can carry you the length of a football field without seeing the road.

questions.prompt: A driver convicted of DUI in Florida can face:
choices: Fines, possible jail, and license revocation
No consequences for a first offense
questions.explanation: A DUI conviction carries heavy fines, possible jail time, and revocation of your driving privilege.

questions.prompt: In Florida, when must you turn on your headlights besides nighttime?
choices: Whenever you use your windshield wipers in rain, fog, or smoke
Only when other cars have theirs on
questions.explanation: Florida law requires headlights on whenever your wipers are running in continuous rain, fog, or smoke, and from sunset to sunrise.

questions.prompt: Roads are usually most slippery:
choices: After several hours of heavy rain
During the first few minutes of rain
questions.explanation: Roads are most slippery in the first few minutes of rain, when water mixes with oil and grime on the pavement.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to hydroplane, you should:
choices: Ease off the gas and steer straight
questions.explanation: If you hydroplane, take your foot off the gas and steer straight rather than braking hard, until your tires regain traction.

questions.prompt: In fog, you should use:
questions.explanation: Use low beams in fog because high beams reflect off the fog and make it harder to see.

questions.prompt: On wet roads, you should:
choices: Drive at the normal speed limit
Slow down and increase your following distance
questions.explanation: Wet roads reduce traction and lengthen stopping distance, so slow down and leave more space ahead.

questions.prompt: You must dim your high beams when an oncoming vehicle is within:
questions.explanation: Dim your high beams when you are within 500 feet of an oncoming vehicle to avoid blinding the other driver.

questions.prompt: Overdriving your headlights at night means:
choices: Driving so fast you cannot stop within the distance you can see
Leaving your lights on while parked
Turning your lights off to save power
questions.explanation: Overdriving your headlights means going too fast to stop within the lit distance ahead, which is dangerous at night.

questions.prompt: When following another vehicle at night, you must dim your high beams within:
questions.explanation: Dim your high beams when you are within 300 feet behind another vehicle so you do not blind that driver through their mirrors.

questions.prompt: If heavy rain makes it impossible to see the road, you should:
choices: Speed up to get through it
Pull safely off the road and wait
questions.explanation: If you cannot see well enough to drive safely, pull completely off the road and wait for conditions to improve.

questions.prompt: You may not park within how many feet of a fire hydrant in Florida?
questions.explanation: Parking is prohibited within 15 feet of a fire hydrant so firefighters can reach it in an emergency.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Facing downhill, turn your front wheels toward the curb so the car will not roll into traffic if the brakes fail.

questions.prompt: In Florida, who must wear a seat belt?
choices: All drivers and front-seat passengers, plus all passengers under 18
questions.explanation: Florida requires seat belts for all drivers and front-seat passengers, and for every passenger under 18 in any seat.

questions.prompt: Children age 3 and under in Florida must be secured in:
questions.explanation: Children age 3 and under must ride in a separate, federally approved child safety seat.

questions.prompt: Which of these is an illegal place to park?
questions.explanation: You may not park on a sidewalk, crosswalk, or within an intersection, as it blocks pedestrians and traffic.

questions.prompt: When you leave your parked vehicle, you should:
choices: Set the parking brake, turn off the engine, and remove the key
Leave the doors unlocked and key in
Leave it in neutral with no brake
questions.explanation: Always set the parking brake, shut off the engine, and take the key to keep the vehicle from moving or being stolen.

questions.prompt: Children ages 4 and 5 in Florida must use:
choices: A separate child seat or booster seat
questions.explanation: Children ages 4 and 5 must be secured in a separate child seat or booster seat under Florida law.

questions.prompt: When parking on any hill, you should always:
questions.explanation: Always set the parking brake when parking on an incline to keep the vehicle from rolling.

questions.prompt: Parking in a space reserved for people with disabilities is allowed:
choices: Only with a valid disabled parking permit
If no other space is open
questions.explanation: You may park in a disabled-reserved space only if you display a valid disabled parking permit or plate.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for a learner's license in Florida?
questions.explanation: You may apply for a Florida learner's license at age 15 after passing the required Class E Knowledge Exam.

questions.prompt: How many hours of supervised driving must a learner's permit holder log in Florida?
questions.explanation: Florida requires 50 hours of supervised driving, including 10 hours at night, before getting a full license.

questions.prompt: While driving with a learner's license in Florida, who must be in the front passenger seat?
choices: A licensed driver at least 21 years old
questions.explanation: A learner's license holder must always have a licensed driver at least 21 years old in the front passenger seat.

questions.prompt: How long must you hold a Florida learner's license before getting a full license?
choices: At least 12 months or until age 18
questions.explanation: You must hold the learner's license for at least 12 months, or until your 18th birthday, before getting a full license.

questions.prompt: For the first three months with a learner's license, you may drive only during:
questions.explanation: For the first three months a learner's license holder may drive only during daylight hours, then until 10 p.m. afterward.

questions.prompt: How many questions are on the Florida Class E Knowledge Exam?
questions.explanation: The Class E Knowledge Exam has 50 questions covering traffic laws, safe driving, and road signs.

questions.prompt: What score do you need to pass the Florida Class E Knowledge Exam?
questions.explanation: You must answer at least 80 percent of the questions correctly (40 of 50) to pass the Class E Knowledge Exam.

questions.prompt: After the first three months, a Florida learner's permit holder may drive until:
questions.explanation: After the first three months, a learner's license holder may drive until 10 p.m. with a qualified supervising driver.

questions.prompt: Before getting a Florida learner's permit, first-time teen drivers must complete:
choices: A Traffic Law and Substance Abuse Education (TLSAE) course
questions.explanation: First-time teen drivers must complete the TLSAE course before they can obtain a learner's license in Florida.

questions.prompt: What happens to a Florida driver under 21 who is caught with a BAC of 0.02 percent or higher?
questions.explanation: Under the zero-tolerance law, a driver under 21 with a BAC of 0.02 percent or higher loses their license for six months.

questions.prompt: How does Florida track unsafe driving behavior over time?
choices: A point system on your record
questions.explanation: Florida adds points to your driving record for moving violations, and too many points leads to a license suspension.

questions.prompt: Under Florida's implied-consent law, refusing a lawful breath, blood, or urine test results in:
questions.explanation: Refusing a lawful chemical test under the implied-consent law results in an automatic suspension of your license.

questions.prompt: When a law enforcement officer pulls you over, you should:
choices: Get out and walk to the officer
Pull to the right, stop, and keep your hands visible
Keep driving to a gas station
Speed up to find a safe spot
questions.explanation: Pull to the right, come to a stop, and stay in your vehicle with your hands visible when an officer pulls you over.

questions.prompt: Which documents must you show when a Florida officer requests them during a stop?
choices: Driver license, vehicle registration, and proof of insurance
questions.explanation: You must present your driver license, vehicle registration, and proof of insurance when an officer asks for them.

questions.prompt: What can happen if you accumulate too many points on your Florida driving record?
choices: Your insurance is canceled by the state
questions.explanation: Accumulating too many points within a set time period leads to suspension of your driving privilege.

questions.prompt: Driving in Florida without a valid driver license is:
questions.explanation: Driving without a valid driver license is against the law and can result in fines and other penalties.

questions.prompt: Operating a vehicle in Florida without the required insurance:
choices: Is a violation that can lead to penalties
Is only an issue after a crash
questions.explanation: Florida requires drivers to carry the minimum insurance, and driving without it can lead to fines and license suspension.

questions.prompt: A DUI conviction in Florida can result in:
choices: License revocation, fines, and possible jail
questions.explanation: A DUI conviction can lead to license revocation, substantial fines, and possible jail time.

questions.prompt: Why is safe driving especially important for new and young Florida drivers?
choices: They earn points for good driving
They can lose their driving privilege quickly from violations
Penalties do not apply to them
questions.explanation: New and young drivers can lose their driving privilege quickly through violations, so safe habits matter from day one.
